channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Has Reportedly Already Sold 500,000 Copies on Steam
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6, the next major title for Xbox, is seemingly already performing well on Steam, reportedly selling over 500,000 copies prior to its launch.

Playground Games’ next installment to the highly revered racing series arrives next month on May 19, 2026. To celebrate it’s launch, Xbox has already unvieled limited edition accessories, which includes a controller and headset. Now, we have been informed about it’s sales numbers on one of the platforms.

Forza Horizon 6 Pre-Launch Sales Reportedly Surpass 500,000 on Steam

According to a report by Alinea Analytics, Forza Horizon 6 has already crossed 500,000 copies sold on Steam, around a month ahead of its launch. The report adds that Forza Horizon 6 is close to reaching $30 million on Steam, with regional pricing and different editions of the game taken into account. The game has also seemingly become the “best-selling Forza title on Steam pre-launch,” with more than a month left for devs to drive further sales.

The report also points out all the factors that have helped Forza Horizon 6 sell so many copies on Steam. There were three factors that have appeared to have kicked off the sales, including the map reveal, driving gameplay and game previews appearing. The surge was further boosted by the developers’ reveal of the title’s achievement list. Playground Games and Xbox have yet to officially reveal the sales figures for Forza Horizon 6.

In other news, interested readers can check out Forza Horizon 6’s complete Summer Season Japan map here. Additionally, here are the official PC system requirements for the upcoming title. What are your thoughts on Forza Horizon 6 selling over 500,000 on Steam pre-launch? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Last of Us creator stating Ellie isn’t the only immune person. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Last of Us Creator Reportedly Said Ellie Isn’t The Only Immune Person
item.content:encoded:
The creator of The Last of Us has reportedly said there is a colony of immune people he would like to explore.

Naughty Dog has been in the news recently, thanks to a claim stating that a new Uncharted game is in the works, alongside the upcoming title, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet. Now, some intriguing information has been revealed about their other massive franchise, The Last of Us.

The Last of Us Part 3 Could Explore a Colony of Immune People

In a recent interview with KIWI TALKZ, Gabriel Betancourt, a former lighting artist at Naughty Dog, discussed the concept of additional immune individuals in The Last of Us universe. Betancourt shared insights from his conversation with Neil Druckmann, head of Naughty Dog, about the decision not to make Ellie the sole immune person at the center of the story. Druckmann replied that Ellie isn’t the only one and that “several people are immune.”

Betancourt elaborated on his conversation, revealing that Druckmann mentioned, “there’s a place, there’s a whole congregation of that kind of person.” Druckmann aims to create a “more sophisticated story” featuring “multiple characters” and expanding upon this narrative. While the Naughty Dog lead has not officially commented on this topic yet, if this information is accurate, we may see this as a plot point in the upcoming game of the series, The Last of Us Part 3.

In other news, a former Naughty Dog dev who worked on Uncharted 4 says the first version of the game didn’t go down well. Additionally, The Last of Us Online was canceled a day before the public announcement, after the director spent 7 years on it. What are your thoughts on there being a colony of immune people in The Last of Us universe? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the official reveal of the limited edition Forza Horizon 6-themed Xbox accessories. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Naughty Dog Almost Shut Down Uncharted 4 Because It ‘Sucked’
item.content:encoded:
Uncharted 4 was released to worldwide acclaim and has sold tens of millions of copies. It’s another bona fide great in one of gaming’s most revered series, but a former Naughty Dog dev claims that the game’s initial build was so badly received that funding was nearly pulled for it.

Leading up to Uncharted 4, Naughty Dog had comfortably cemented the franchise as one of PlayStation’s marquee IPs. Uncharted 4 was the series’ debut on the PS4, and it ended up being a technical marvel. According to a former Naughty Dog dev, this was far from the case at first.

Uncharted 4 Implied To Have ‘Sucked’ And Had To Be Remade

Gabriel Betancourt has worked for Monarch Games Inc. for the last seven years, primarily focusing on Technical, Lighting, Game Design, and App Development.

But if we turn back the clock, he worked for Naughty Dog between July 2012 and February 2019.

His work includes The Last of Us, The Last of Us Part 2, Uncharted: Lost Legacy, The Last of Us: Left Behind DLC, and crucially, Uncharted 4.

In an interview with KIWI TALKZ, Betancourt opened up on (we believe) Uncharted 4’s early days of development:

“I remember this one game prototype, and I won’t say which one, but there was a project that had been in production for two and a half years almost three, and corporate had said ‘we’re having questions about this.’ The franchise was successful up until that point, there was no reason to question that this thing would be successful.

But at that time, the leader was surrounded by people who were always saying yes, and they were living in the glory of their success going onto their fourth project, and now they’re delivering this deadline for a demo, and I think they just took it for granted. We’ve already had success before, we’re gonna have it again, we’ve done this three of four times already, so we’re just gonna be this great thing.

One of the directors from another team was asked to weigh in, and I wasn’t there, I only heard the story, and that director walked in, in the room full of all these people, he picked up the controller, and he said to everyone: ‘This game sucks’.

Betancourt explained that this left the entire room in shock, the project’s director (presumably Amy Hennig) was angry, and when someone working on the game outed the comments as ‘rude,’ the other director responded:

“No, but the game sucks, there’s no objectives, it’s hard to follow what’s going on, the controls are clunky, this isn’t like you guys, this isn’t what we delivered in the past. In the meeting, people walked out of there really upset. This director, I’m surprised he had the gaul to say something like that.”

Things didn’t stop there, though, as Betancourt revealed that higher-ups looked at the project, and at first, they were continuing the game’s development, and would ‘ignore the naysayer.’ But then he added that corporate took things further:

You guys are in trouble. We’re gonna pull funding from this if you guys don’t fix this. As a result, the director of that project was pulled away, even thought they had been successful, and the naysayer, along with two others, were put in to help fix it, and then game had to be redone. Then, two and a half to three years later, so five to six years total production, the game became a success.

Now, Uncharted 4 is never referenced at any point in this story; however, based on the events and the timeline, it’s clear that the game is Uncharted 4. During Betancourt’s time at Naughty Dog, the only series to get a fourth main entry was Uncharted.

Crash Team Racing (which was ND’s fourth Crash game) was well before his employment with the company, and Jak X—which was technically the fourth game in the franchise—was also before the developer arrived. Furthermore, Amy Hennig left Naughty Dog in 2014, which was roughly two to three years before Uncharted 4’s release.

Everything clearly points to Uncharted 4 being the game, and it’s unbelievable to think that Uncharted 4 was going to be shelved at one point!

This is one of the wilder stories we’ve heard recently. What do you make of it? Are you glad things were turned around? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out Shuhei Yoshida saying he was removed as PlayStation President because he ‘Didn’t Listen’ to Jim Ryan for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sjokz Shares that “Abuse is Getting a Little Out of Hand” as Co-Streamer Debate Turns Toxic
item.content:encoded:
Eefje ‘Sjokz’ Depoortere has shared on social media that the “abuse” received regarding the League of Legends co-streaming debate has gotten “out of hand.”

The post seemed to appeal for a cooling of the temperature surrounding the debate about co-streaming and its effects on the ecosystem, which has seen increasingly toxic online posts in recent days.

The Belgian broadcast legend reiterated her appreciation of popular co-streamer Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont before “My words are getting warped for things I did not say, and the abuse is getting a little out of hand.”

Adding, “If you’re looking for someone who has been dedicated for 15 years to trying to make any broadcast she is part of better and put the viewers first, I’m right here. I am not your enemy.”

Sjokz co-created and co-hosts The League Awards with Caedrel, an annual awards show that celebrates global League of Legends esports. So far, there have been two ceremonies, in 2024 and 2025.

What is the co-streaming controversy?

The debate surrounding how beneficial or detrimental co-streaming is to League of Legends or other esports has been a significant topic over the last few years as the trend continues to capture a larger portion of the viewership.

A report by Stream Hatchet shared that co-streaming had surpassed the official broadcast in viewership as of Q2 of 2025, drawing into question whether official broadcasts are under threat due to co-streaming or whether they were propped up by it.

The debate has once again been rekindled within the League of Legends community, but strangely did not even begin in LoL circles, but proliferated from new co-streaming rules enacted by ESL for their Counter-Strike and Dota 2 events.

Amongst other provisions, the rules demanded “Always-on Coverage.” This means that co-streamers of ESL events will be required to watch the entire product, not switch to anything other than the tournament, and make sure the broadcast can be heard at all times.

The rules received a mixed reaction from fans and streamers, with some praising the rules for maintaining the original broadcast, and some fans of co-streamers disliking the rules and saying it will impact viewership.

Sjokz was one of several broadcast talent to praise the new set of regulations, stating that the rules, “Should be the standard for ALL COSTREAMERS across esports imo. Watching the broadcast in full at the very least.”

That is a sentiment Sjokz reiterated in her recent post, stating, “I support and understand a broadcaster wanting their product shown.”

FlyQuest video further agitates co-streaming debate

That discourse was quickly followed by the release of a YouTube video entitled “Did Content Creators Bankrupt Esports” by FlyQuest in conjunction with YouTuber turndownforwalt, which discusses co-streaming as part of a promotion of FlyQuest’s content creators.

The video caused backlash from fans and content creators, such as Cloud9 coach and co-streamer Christian ‘IWDominate’ Rivera, who labelled the video “a steaming pile of dogs**t.”

Caedrel also attacked the video, claiming it was capitalizing on broader discussion about co-streamers for the org’s benefit, calling it “shameless” and “ragebait.”

The overall effect of the video was to raise an already heightened social media furore surrounding the debate over co-streaming and its effects on the industry, culminating in further ad hominem attacks on social media, ultimately leading to Sjokz’s statement.

While the debate continues to rumble on, it is really in Riot’s actions that we will see the economics of co-streaming truly play out. ESL has clearly made the decision that the official broadcast must be maintained and that advertising must not be obstructed. Will Riot make the same moves?

What are your views on co-streaming in the League of Legends space? You can discuss that on our Discord!
For other League news, see more about the high-flying Vitality roster.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/sjokz-co-streaming-debate-explained/

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Xbox Accessories Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has revealed Forza Horizon 6-themed limited-edition accessories: an Xbox Wireless Controller and a Headset.

Thanks to a report from a reliable insider earlier this month, players already knew that they were getting limited-edition accessories for the release of Forza Horizon 6. Now, Xbox has given players official confirmation, along with a look at the designs of the new trinkets.

Forza Horizon 6 Limited Edition Xbox Wireless Controller and Headset

Starting with the Forza Horizon 6 Limited Edition Xbox Wireless Controller, it features a translucent top case and triggers with a race track stretching across its body. The Xbox logo features dynamic lighting effects of vibrant pink and green; the thumbsticks are bold pink. The ABXY buttons are multi-coloured, and the Horizon Festival logo has been slapped on the battery door.

Then we have the new headset, which features spatial sound technologies like Windows Sonic, Dolby Atmos, and DTS Headphone: X. The translucent theme extends to the headset’s blue boom mic, which also features a Forza logo with hot pink metallic backing. The adjustable headband also features a bold “HORIZON” graphic, and the festive-style earcup dials have silver accents.

Check out the images of the new Forza Horizon 6 Limited Edition Xbox Accessories below:

The new accessories were revealed through a post on the official Xbox X account (formerly Twitter). The new CEO, Asha Shar, commented on the limited edition accessories, stating, “Everything about Forza Horizon 6 is beautiful. The accessories had to be too.”

In other news, the ID@Xbox Showcase is returning this week, with a focus on games like Aphelion, Solo Leveling Arise Overdrive, and more. Additionally, the Xbox Game Pass additions for April 2026’s Wave 2 have been revealed, and 10 games will leave the service this month. What are your thoughts on Limited Edition Forza Horizon 6-themed Xbox accessories? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Crimson Desert actor admitting the game ‘lacks in narrative’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/forza-horizon-6-xbox-accessories-officially-revealed/

item.title: Crimson Desert Actor Admits Game ‘Lacks in Narrative’
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert has been on the market for just over a month. It dropped midway through March and impressed players with a massive, open-world environment with plenty to uncover, but many felt the game’s story was somewhat lacking. It drove down some review scores, with some writing that the game was inconsistent and missing some depth.

In a recent AMA on Reddit, the mocap actor and likeness behind Kliff, the game’s protagonist, became the latest to acknowledge the game’s holes.

Crimson Desert Has Plenty of Meat

Crimson Desert has gone on to sell millions of copies, and Pearl Abyss is busy pouring quality-of-life improvements into the game to address some of the more glaring issues. Many have argued that if the game had been released now, enough has already changed that it would garner stronger reviews.

However, one thing that can’t really be changed is the game’s core campaign. It’s lengthy and spread over many chapters, but some feel it’s inconsistent, has poor pacing, and suffers from a lack of depth.

That was alluded to by Trevor McEwan during a recent AMA on Reddit. McEwan is a performance capture actor and provided the motion and face for Kliff, Crimson Desert’s gruff lead, voiced by Alec Newman.

When asked how he feels about the plot of Crimson Desert, McEwan transparently said:

I think what it lacks in narrative it more than makes up for in other aspects. There’s also lore hidden across the world as well, if players don’t mind a bit of reading.

(Thanks to GamesRadar for the spot)

McEwan also revealed that he worked on the motion capture and stunts alongside a Korean team, which was responsible for securing the more ‘dangerous’ sequences. He contributed mostly to the cinematics and cutscenes that try to flesh out that fledgling story.

Admittedly, he said that Crimson Desert turned out ‘better than (he) had hoped,’ suggesting that while it’s not perfect, it did exceed expectations. Even the switch from an MMO model to a single-player game didn’t impact his role too much:

Other changes affected the overall story mostly. Kliff stayed Kliff throughout development for the most part.

Have you got around to playing Crimson Desert yet? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox Indie Showcase Announced for This Week
item.content:encoded:
The ID@Xbox Showcase is back this week, with the livestream on April 23 at 10 AM PT.

Xbox has been gearing up for a bunch of showcases in the coming weeks. Just last month, we saw the announcement of Xbox Games Showcase 2026 for June, which would be followed by Gears of War E-Day Direct. However, players can have a taste of a smaller showcase this week as they wait for the big one.

ID@Xbox Showcase Features Apehlion, Solo Leveling Arise Overdrive and More

A new report by IGN reveals the date and time of the upcoming ID@Xbox Showcase, along with details on which games will be featured. ID@Xbox is an indie-centric event, so players will see several hit indie titles featured. From what has been announced, Mistfall Hunter, There Are No Ghosts at the Grand, Aphelion, and Solo Leveling Arise Overdrive will definitely be included, with more to be revealed on the day of the event.

The last ID@Xbox showcase was held in October 2025, and we saw several big indie games highlighted at the event. Some of the games featured at the previous event will arrive as Day One additions to Xbox Game Pass in April 2026 Wave 2, including Aphelion and Sledding Game. Other highlights included the likes of TCG Pocket Simulator, Mouse: P.I. For Hire, Invincible VS, and more. Let’s see what the 2026 iteration has in store for us this Thursday.

In other news, here’s everything that was announced during the third Galaxies Gaming Showcase. Additionally, Xbox users will soon be able to redeem Microsoft Rewards points directly on the console. Will you be tuning in for the ID@Xbox Showcase on April 23? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Escape from Tarkov receiving another meta change from the latest patch. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/xbox-indie-showcase-announced-for-this-week/

item.title: Is Invincible VS On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch? All Console Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fighting games have always played best with a controller, and that’s made consoles the perfect home for the genre. Now, with Invincible VS fast approaching, it’s got fans questioning which platforms it’ll be playable on.

18 characters will be available at launch, and the game’s set to bring the same level of violence and gore as the show and the comics. It looks like a perfect treat for fans of the franchise, but if you want to play, you’d better make sure it’s releasing on your console of choice.

For all the details surrounding which platforms Invincible VS is releasing on, Insider Gaming has got you covered.

Is Invincible VS On PlayStation?

Good news, PlayStation fans, as Invincible VS will be launching on the PS5 on release day.

When it comes to consoles, PlayStation has to have the most diverse library out there. From its suite of generation-defining first-party titles to its wide range of third-party games, if a game’s coming to console, it usually winds up on the PlayStation 5.

  • More Insider Gaming: Invincible VS Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

And the same can be said for Invincible VS. The 3v3 tag-fighter is heading straight to PS5s on launch day, meaning any PlayStation fan can start squaring up to Conquest the moment the game drops—but don’t let it be a mistake!

Is Invincible VS On Xbox?

Don’t worry, Xbox fans, you aren’t being left out, as Invincible VS will be launching on both the Xbox Series X|S.

It’s not always a guarantee that a third-party game on PlayStation will also arrive on Xbox, but thankfully, it’s the case with Invincible VS. Better yet, there will be seamless crossplay between each console, so there’s no excuse for your friends to turn down your battle requests.

Is Invincible VS On Nintendo Switch?

This is where the bad news arrives: sorry, Nintendo Switch users, Invincible VS will not be playable on any Nintendo Switch consoles at launch.

Whilst it may not arrive at launch, that’s not to say that it couldn’t appear in the future. Nintendo is constantly bringing new titles onto its system, with Cyberpunk 2077 and Resident Evil 9 being two shining examples.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is NTE: Neverness to Everness on PlayStation, Xbox & PC?

If they can make those work on a Switch 2, there’s always hope that Invincible VS could be ported, too. But as things stand, if you want to play Invincible VS on a console at launch, you’ll need access to a PS5 or Xbox Series X|S.

What platform would you want to play Invincible VS on? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platform guides, check out the following:

  • Forza Horizon 6
  • Saros
  • Windrose

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2 Titles Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The second Wave of games coming to Xbox Game Pass in April 2026 has been revealed, including Aphelion, Kiln, and more.

We saw some really big names added to Xbox Game Pass in Wave 1, including Hades 2, Replaced, Football Manager 26, and more. Wave 2 doesn’t have the biggest names; however, there is a good collection of titles for players to try out. The games will be added from April 21 to May 5, 2026.

Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2 Features Final Fantasy 5, Aphelion, and More

As mentioned above, April 2026 Wave 2 for Xbox Game Pass doesn’t have the flashiest titles, but it does have a fair few for players to spend their time on. Players will get to explore several Day One titles, including Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivor, Kiln, Aphelion, and more. There’s a classic entry reserved for this Wave as well, with Final Fantasy 5 arriving on May 5. Check out all the games included in Wave 2 below:

  • Little Rocket Lab (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 21
  • Sopa: Tale of the Stolen Potato (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – April 21
  • Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 21 (Day One)
  • Kiln (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 23 (Day One)
  • Aphelion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 28 (Day One)
  • Trepang2 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 29
  • Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era (Game Preview) (PC) – April 30 (Day One)
  • Sledding Game (Game Preview) (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30 (Day One)
  • TerraTech Legion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30
  • Final Fantasy V (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – May 5

Xbox Game Pass Games Leaving in April

As and when we see new games being added to the service, we also see some games leave the collection. Players will see 10 games removed from Xbox Game Pass on April 30, including Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2, Goat Simulator Remastered, and more. Check out the full list below:

  • Citizen Sleeper (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Creatures of Ava (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Endless Legend 2 (PC)
  • Goat Simulator (Cloud and Console)
  • Goat Simulator Remastered (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Hunt Showdown 1896 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • NHL 24 (EA Play) (Cloud and Console)
  • Revenge of the Savage Planet (Cloud, Console, and PC)

The list of games that were added and will leave Game Pass in April was revealed in a post on Xbox Wire.

In other news, Xbox’s rumoured first-party tier will support cloud gaming, but will feature monthly time limits. Additionally, CEO Asha Sharma admitted that Xbox Game Pass is too expensive for players. What are your thoughts on the Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2 additions? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Tarkov Arena’s biggest patch yet. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
